acceptanceCriteria: bash scripts/verify.sh passes (pytest + ruff + mypy via .venv)
All non-skipped features have passes: true
notes: ellip2 Bitcoin/Elliptic2 AML pipeline. Master spec = plan.md (esp. 'Resolved design decisions', repo layout sec 6, pipeline sec 9). Rules = plans/guardrails.md. Each feature: create the module under src/ellip2/<pkg>/ (add __init__.py if new), write a synthetic/CPU/mocked test tests/test_<name>.py (follow tests/test_ingest.py), keep ruff+mypy clean. Dependency-ordered; high=Stage1, medium=Stage2, low=Stage3/LLM/config. NO GitHub: commit as 'feat: [T-00X] desc' with no Fixes #N.

features.title: features/degree.py — per-cluster degree features
acceptanceCriteria: src/ellip2/features/degree.py computes in_degree, out_degree, total_degree, in_out_ratio per cluster from edge_index (the Stage 0 (2,E) int32 array)
Returns a per-cluster table sized N_nodes; isolated nodes get degree 0
tests/test_degree.py builds a tiny hand-counted directed graph and asserts exact degree values and the in_out_ratio convention for zero denominators
bash scripts/verify.sh green
features.notes: plan.md sec 4 (Degree). Use numpy bincount over edge_index rows (no pandas). Define in_out_ratio for out_degree==0 explicitly and test it. Establish the features/ package + test idiom here.

features.title: features/edge_aggs.py — per-cluster edge-feature aggregates
acceptanceCriteria: src/ellip2/features/edge_aggs.py computes sum/mean/max/std of edge features per cluster, separately for in-edges and out-edges
Driven by DuckDB group-by over background_edges joined via id_map (out-of-core, no pandas), or over a provided edge-feature array in the test
tests/test_edge_aggs.py asserts aggregates vs hand-computed values on a tiny synthetic edge table
bash scripts/verify.sh green
features.notes: plan.md sec 4 (Edge-feature aggregates). Keep the DuckDB streaming pattern from ingest.py. 95 anonymized edge feature columns — accept configurable indices, default to first few for the test.

features.title: features/flow_concentration.py — Gini/HHI/max-counterparty
acceptanceCriteria: src/ellip2/features/flow_concentration.py computes per cluster the Gini and HHI of incoming and outgoing edge weights, plus max-single-counterparty share
tests/test_flow_concentration.py asserts Gini/HHI vs hand-computed values (equal weights -> Gini 0; one dominant -> near 1)
bash scripts/verify.sh green
features.notes: plan.md sec 4 (Flow concentration). Surfaces peeling-chain structure. Pure-numpy Gini; document the formula.

features.title: features/neighborhood.py — leakage-masked neighbor label fractions
acceptanceCriteria: src/ellip2/features/neighborhood.py computes 1-hop and 2-hop neighbor label fractions (licit/suspicious/unknown) per cluster via scipy.sparse propagation
Leakage mask EXCLUDES the cluster's own subgraph label and EXCLUDES TEST-split subgraph labels (consume the persisted split from ellip2.eval.splits)
tests/test_neighborhood.py asserts fractions on a tiny graph AND asserts the leakage invariants (own-label and test-split labels never leak)
bash scripts/verify.sh green
features.notes: plan.md sec 4 + sec 7. SIGN-103 mandatory. Build adjacency as scipy.sparse from edge_index; propagate label indicator vectors. The leakage test is the key assertion.

features.title: features/temporal.py — activity span & burstiness
acceptanceCriteria: src/ellip2/features/temporal.py computes activity span and a burstiness measure per cluster from edge timestamps
tests/test_temporal.py asserts span and burstiness on a synthetic timestamp sequence with a known answer
bash scripts/verify.sh green
features.notes: plan.md sec 4 (Temporal). Burstiness B=(sigma-mu)/(sigma+mu) of inter-event times is a fine testable choice — document it.

features.title: features/path_role.py — source/sink (endpoint) heuristic
acceptanceCriteria: src/ellip2/features/path_role.py scores each cluster on a source-vs-sink / exchange-like axis from degree + cluster size + throughput
Output is clearly labeled a DERIVED heuristic, not a ground-truth entity type
tests/test_path_role.py asserts high-in-degree+large-size clusters score endpoint/exchange-like and low ones do not, on synthetic inputs
bash scripts/verify.sh green
features.notes: plan.md Resolved decision #3 + sec 4. This is the 'licit endpoint' heuristic used by Stage 3. Monotone, documented; calibrate by percentile.

features.title: features/build.py + scripts/build_features.py — assemble cluster_features.parquet
acceptanceCriteria: src/ellip2/features/build.py joins all per-feature tables (T-001..T-006) into one per-cluster frame and writes cluster_features.parquet (one row per cluster idx)
scripts/build_features.py is a thin CLI over the ingest artifacts dir
tests/test_build_features.py runs full assembly on the tiny synthetic ingest output and asserts combined schema + row count == N_nodes + no NaNs in required columns
bash scripts/verify.sh green
features.notes: plan.md sec 9 Stage 1. Reuse the Stage 0 synthetic-CSV fixtures (import helpers from tests/test_ingest.py) to produce artifacts, then build features over them. Depends on T-001..T-006.

features.title: graph/pyg_data.py — homogeneous PyG Data from memmaps
acceptanceCriteria: src/ellip2/graph/pyg_data.py builds a torch_geometric.data.Data (single node type, single edge type) from edge_index.npy + node_features.npy plus subgraph membership accessors
Loads arrays via numpy memmap (mmap_mode='r')
tests/test_pyg_data.py asserts Data.x shape (N,43), edge_index shape (2,E) dtype long, and a subgraph's member indices round-trip
bash scripts/verify.sh green
features.notes: plan.md sec 3. CPU torch. Homogeneous Data, NOT HeteroData. Full-graph GNN out of scope.

features.title: pu/nnpu_loss.py — non-negative PU risk (Kiryo 2017)
acceptanceCriteria: src/ellip2/pu/nnpu_loss.py implements R = pi_p*E_P[l+] + max(0, E_U[l-] - pi_p*E_P[l-]); configurable prior and surrogate loss; returns a torch scalar with grad
tests/test_nnpu_loss.py asserts the risk vs a hand value on toy logits AND asserts the non-negative clamp engages when the unbiased term goes negative
bash scripts/verify.sh green
features.notes: plan.md sec 2; arXiv:1703.00593. CPU torch. Pure deterministic function — make the clamp explicit in a test.

features.title: pu/prior_estimation.py — class-prior estimation + sweep
acceptanceCriteria: src/ellip2/pu/prior_estimation.py implements at least one mixture-proportion estimator (KM2 or TIcE) plus a sweep-grid helper (e.g. 1e-1..1e-5)
tests/test_prior_estimation.py builds a synthetic P/U mixture with a KNOWN prior and asserts recovery within tolerance; asserts the sweep grid is well-formed
bash scripts/verify.sh green
features.notes: plan.md Resolved decision #2. pi_p is the CLUSTER-level prior (small), NOT the 2.27% subgraph base rate.

features.title: pu/encoder.py — heterophily-tolerant GNN encoder
acceptanceCriteria: src/ellip2/pu/encoder.py defines a GraphSAGE/GIN-style encoder separating ego vs neighbor aggregation; CPU forward on a tiny PyG graph
tests/test_encoder.py asserts output embedding shape and differentiability (loss.backward populates grads)
bash scripts/verify.sh green
features.notes: plan.md sec 2/3. Avoid pure-GCN smoothing; include ego features. Small + CPU-runnable.

features.title: graph/neighbor_sampling.py — NeighborLoader wrapper
acceptanceCriteria: src/ellip2/graph/neighbor_sampling.py wraps NeighborLoader with configurable num_neighbors (default [15,10]) and batch size
tests/test_neighbor_sampling.py samples a tiny graph and asserts batches have expected seeds and respect fanout caps
bash scripts/verify.sh green
features.notes: plan.md sec 3. Sample only the candidate set, never a full-graph GNN. CPU.

features.title: pu/trainer.py — supervised subgraph baseline + nnPU head + max-pool
acceptanceCriteria: src/ellip2/pu/trainer.py trains (a) DEFAULT supervised subgraph model: DeepSets(senders) + DeepSets(receivers) + pooled internal node/edge feats -> MLP with weighted BCE; and (b) optional cluster-level nnPU head
Provides max-pool of cluster scores -> subgraph score (MIL: bag positive iff >=1 positive member)
Checkpoint save/load round-trips (model+optimizer)
tests/test_trainer.py CPU smoke-trains a few steps on synthetic data, asserts loss decreases, checkpoint round-trips, and cluster->subgraph max-pool matches a hand example
bash scripts/verify.sh green
features.notes: plan.md Resolved decisions #1 and #2 + sec 4 (border-set = RevClassify insight). Subgraph = supervised by default; nnPU = cluster-level option. CPU smoke test only — no GPU/real data/S3. Depends on T-008/T-009/T-011.

features.title: eval/pu_metrics.py — PR-AUC, F1, recall, Lee&Liu PU metric
acceptanceCriteria: src/ellip2/eval/pu_metrics.py implements PR-AUC, binary F1 on the suspicious class, recall on held-out positives, and Lee&Liu recall^2 / Pr(predicted positive)
tests/test_pu_metrics.py asserts each metric vs hand-computed values on small score/label vectors
bash scripts/verify.sh green
features.notes: plan.md sec 7. Reporting metrics vs RevClassify (name the table when quoting). Deterministic + testable.

features.title: eval/leakage_checks.py — leakage invariant assertions
acceptanceCriteria: src/ellip2/eval/leakage_checks.py asserts: no test-split subgraph label in any training feature; prior/training/recall positive splits are disjoint; background features do not encode subgraph membership
tests/test_leakage_checks.py constructs a clean case (passes) and a leaky case (raises) for each invariant
bash scripts/verify.sh green
features.notes: plan.md sec 7; pairs with SIGN-103. Reusable guards the feature/training tasks call.

features.title: exit_paths/path_search.py — bounded reachability (backward+forward BFS)
acceptanceCriteria: src/ellip2/exit_paths/path_search.py runs backward multi-source BFS from an endpoint set on the transposed adjacency (scipy.sparse boolean SpMV) AND forward BFS from candidate sources, each with per-level frontier caps and hubs excluded from pass-through; intersects within k<=6
Returns candidate->endpoint reachability (NOT full path enumeration); optional induced-subgraph extraction on survivors
tests/test_path_search.py asserts on a tiny graph: correct reachable set within k hops, frontier cap respected, hub not used as transit (stopped at), and backward∩forward correctness
bash scripts/verify.sh green
features.notes: plan.md Resolved decision #4 + sec 3. Reachability not enumeration. Endpoints from T-006 heuristic. Pure scipy.sparse — CPU, no cuGraph for the test.

features.title: scripts/discover.py — rank candidates with corroboration
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/discover.py wires top-percentile PU scores -> path_search exit paths -> corroboration filter (high score AND valid <=6-hop exit path AND typology signal) -> ranked candidates
tests/test_discover.py runs the pipeline on synthetic scores+graph and asserts only corroborated candidates above the percentile threshold are returned, ranked
bash scripts/verify.sh green
features.notes: plan.md sec 9 Stage 3 + sec 7 (RevFilter corroboration; Hit-Rate/NDCG-style ranking). Depends on T-016 and scores.

features.title: llm/serialize_subgraph.py — compact JSON serialization
acceptanceCriteria: src/ellip2/llm/serialize_subgraph.py serializes a candidate subgraph to compact JSON: nodes (role + binned features), directed edges (weight/volume/timestamp), exit path(s), degree/Gini/flow stats, PU score
Enforces a configurable size budget deterministically
tests/test_serialize_subgraph.py asserts schema, determinism, and that the size budget is respected
bash scripts/verify.sh green
features.notes: plan.md sec 8. Pure function, no network. Determinism is the key testable property.

features.title: llm/bedrock_client.py — Converse wrapper (injectable client)
acceptanceCriteria: src/ellip2/llm/bedrock_client.py wraps Bedrock Converse; the boto3 client is INJECTABLE so tests pass a stub — NO real network, NO real AWS creds
Parses a Converse response into {typology, confidence, rationale, evidence}
tests/test_bedrock_client.py injects a stub client returning a canned Converse payload and asserts parsing + that no default boto3 client is constructed in the test
bash scripts/verify.sh green
features.notes: plan.md sec 8. SIGN-101: never hit the network. Model id is a configurable string; do not call it in tests. claude-api skill is the reference for real Claude/Bedrock model ids (not needed for the mock).

features.title: llm/typology_graph.py — LangGraph agent (mock LLM node)
acceptanceCriteria: src/ellip2/llm/typology_graph.py builds a LangGraph state machine: serialize -> classify (injectable LLM node) -> validate (rule-based vs structural signals) -> report
tests/test_typology_graph.py runs the graph end-to-end with a MOCK LLM node and asserts state transitions and that rule-based validation can override/flag a contradicted typology
bash scripts/verify.sh green
features.notes: plan.md sec 8. Typologies: peeling chain, nested service, layering/smurfing, consolidation. Mock the LLM; test wiring + validation. Depends on T-018/T-019.

features.title: report/render.py — per-subgraph report
acceptanceCriteria: src/ellip2/report/render.py renders a per-subgraph report: id, PU score + percentile, exit path(s) to heuristic licit endpoint type, typology + confidence, structural evidence (degree/Gini/flow), LLM rationale, and a false-positive caveat block
tests/test_render.py asserts all required fields including the caveat block on a synthetic candidate
bash scripts/verify.sh green
features.notes: plan.md sec 8. The caveat block is mandatory — outputs are investigative leads needing human review.

features.title: configs/ — Hydra config tree
acceptanceCriteria: configs/ contains composable Hydra groups dataset/, features/, pu/, gnn/, llm/, infra/ with sane defaults
tests/test_configs.py loads/composes the configs and asserts key fields resolve (or validates YAML loads + required keys if hydra is not added)
bash scripts/verify.sh green
features.notes: plan.md sec 6. If adding hydra-core/omegaconf, install via uv into .venv and add to the dev extra. Keep the gate green.

features.title: infra/userdata.sh Stages 1-3 + S3 checkpoint/resume + IMDS spot handler
features.skipReason: Integration task requiring AWS (EC2/S3/IMDS) — cannot be verified by offline CPU unit tests. Implement manually once the pipeline modules exist.
features.notes: plan.md sec 5. Human/integration pass; not part of the autonomous loop.

features.title: Real-data end-to-end on EC2 g5 + GPU
features.skipReason: Requires the 26 GB Elliptic2 download and an A10G GPU — out of scope for offline CPU verification.
features.notes: plan.md sec 9. Run after modules are built and Dockerized; validate against RevClassify PR-AUC/F1 on the shipped split.

features.title: Per-cluster HGBM suspicion scorer (cluster_score)
acceptanceCriteria: ellip2.pu.cluster_score: train main fits HistGradientBoostingClassifier on cluster_features rows (load_features X) vs positive_mask over LABELED members of the train split (suspicious-subgraph members=positive, licit-subgraph members=negative), reports test-member PR-AUC, pickles the model; score main writes cluster_scores.npy (N,) over all clusters (chunked, lean load).
scripts/train_cluster.py + scripts/score_cluster.py thin shims.
tests/test_cluster_score.py: synthetic separable clusters -> train->score end-to-end, high held-out PR-AUC, scores.npy shape (N,).
bash scripts/verify.sh green
features.notes: Reuse ellip2.pu.train.load_features + positive_mask, train_subgraph.py HGBM pattern, eval.pu_metrics. Column-at-a-time lean load (subgraph_pool.py). Plan: ~/.claude/plans/yes-lets-do-it-glittery-coral.md

features.title: Typology-signal export + known-member exclusion helpers
acceptanceCriteria: A helper exports the source_sink_axis column of cluster_features to a (N,) .npy typology signal (Gate 3); a helper returns the union of all labeled-subgraph member idxs (exclusion set) from subgraphs.parquet.
tests: typology signal aligned to idx; exclusion union correct.
bash scripts/verify.sh green
features.notes: Mirror ellip2.exit_paths.recover.endpoints_from_features. Exclusion via np.unique(concat(member_idx)) or positive_mask(all-labels).

features.title: Per-candidate subgraph carve (reachability)
acceptanceCriteria: ellip2.discovery.background.candidate_member_sets(edge_index, candidate_ids, endpoints, n_nodes, *, max_hops, frontier_cap, hubs): build directed adjacency once, bfs_reachable BACKWARD from endpoints once (global), per-candidate FORWARD bfs, carve survivors=fwd.reached & back.reached & (fwd.hops+back.hops<=max_hops); return {cand: member_idx array} with endpoint ids dropped.
tests/test_background_discovery.py: synthetic graph -> carve equals known set; respects max_hops (unreachable -> empty).
bash scripts/verify.sh green
features.notes: Reuse ellip2.exit_paths.path_search.bfs_reachable + induced_subgraph. Global backward reused; only forward per-candidate.

features.title: Background discovery orchestrator (discover_background)
acceptanceCriteria: ellip2.discovery.background.discover_background(...): Gate1 score>=percentile EXCLUDING known members, take top-K; per-candidate carve (Gate2 reaches endpoint=non-empty carve, Gate3 typology>=threshold); border-score each member-set ONE-AT-A-TIME with a node-only SupervisedSubgraphModel checkpoint; rank by border score; write discovered_subgraphs.parquet (ccId,ccLabel,n_members,member_idx) + discovered_scores.parquet (ccId,score,label,split).
scripts/discover_subgraphs.py shim.
test: synthetic end-to-end -> novel candidate surfaced, known members excluded, member sets valid, outputs in existing schema.
bash scripts/verify.sh green
features.notes: Reuse candidate_member_sets(T-027), extract_border_sets/build_subgraph_batch/SupervisedSubgraphModel/load_checkpoint (score_border.py template). Emit existing schema so leads/investigate reuse unchanged.

features.title: Optimize per-candidate reachability (reuse transposed step matrix)
acceptanceCriteria: bfs_reachable (ellip2.exit_paths.path_search) accepts an optional precomputed step_matrix (== adjacency.transpose().tocsr()); when provided it SKIPS the per-call transpose (path_search.py:198) and uses it directly.

candidate_member_sets (ellip2.discovery.background) builds the transposed CSR ONCE and passes it as step_matrix to the single global backward sweep AND to every per-candidate forward sweep, so the O(nnz) transpose happens once, not per candidate.

Results IDENTICAL to before: a test asserts equal member sets with vs without the optimization, and that candidate_member_sets does not re-transpose per candidate (monkeypatch a transpose counter or equivalent structural assertion).
bash scripts/verify.sh green
features.notes: Root cause: bfs_reachable does adjacency.transpose().tocsr() EVERY call; candidate_member_sets calls it per candidate -> ~500x transpose of the 196M-nnz matrix (~49min real run). Forward step_matrix == a_t (already built for backward). Keep the public bfs_reachable API back-compatible (step_matrix optional).

features.title: Held-out-recovery proxy eval harness
acceptanceCriteria: ellip2.discovery.eval_recovery: recovery_rate(discovered_member_sets, target_member_sets) -> fraction of TARGET subgraphs with >=1 member in any discovered member set, plus the random-baseline expectation (given |discovered union| / N). A CLI reads discovered_subgraphs.parquet + subgraphs.parquet + split.csv and reports recovery of the eval-split SUSPICIOUS subgraphs vs baseline. + scripts/eval_recovery.py shim.

discover_main / known_member_idx gain an optional split filter (e.g. --exclude-split train) so discovery can EXCLUDE only train-split members while leaving held-out (test-suspicious) clusters seedable.
tests/test_eval_recovery.py: recovery metric + baseline on synthetic; split-filtered exclusion excludes only the named split's members.
bash scripts/verify.sh green
features.notes: Proxy: cluster scorer trains on train-split members (test held out); run discovery excluding ONLY train members so test-suspicious clusters can be seeded; measure how many test-suspicious subgraphs are recovered by discovered candidates vs random. Label-free yardstick. The real eval RUN is a manual box step after the loop.

features.title: README.md — project front door
acceptanceCriteria: README.md at repo root with these ## sections: pitch/overview, Results (detection progression + named-table baseline comparison), Pipeline (ASCII diagram of Stage 0->discovery), Quickstart (uv/.venv install + `bash scripts/verify.sh`/pytest), Data (Kaggle source, ~83 GB extracted, CC BY-NC-ND 4.0 non-commercial), Reproduce (pointer to RUNBOOK), Repo layout (src/ellip2 modules + scripts CLIs), Limitations, License (MIT), Citation (both arXiv ids).
tests/test_docs.py: asserts README.md exists, contains each required section header, states the correct data size (contains '83' and NOT '26 GB'/'26GB'), and links RESULTS.md.
bash scripts/verify.sh green
features.notes: ALL numbers come from plans/publishing_facts.md (READ IT FIRST — single source of truth; do not invent metrics). Border model = Deep Sets over senders/receivers + pooled internal features. Keep it scannable; tables > prose for metrics.

features.title: LICENSE (MIT) + data-license note
acceptanceCriteria: LICENSE file at root = standard MIT text, year 2026, holder from git identity (owgreen-dev).
A prominent note (README Data section and/or DATA.md) states the Elliptic2 DATA is CC BY-NC-ND 4.0 (non-commercial, no-derivatives) and is NOT redistributed here; code is MIT.
tests/test_docs.py: LICENSE exists and contains 'MIT'; the non-commercial data note ('CC BY-NC-ND' or 'non-commercial') is present in a tracked doc.
bash scripts/verify.sh green
features.notes: ALL numbers come from plans/publishing_facts.md (READ IT FIRST — single source of truth; do not invent metrics).

features.title: RESULTS.md — metrics + baseline comparison
acceptanceCriteria: RESULTS.md with: detection progression table (0.030->0.286->0.816->0.844->0.942), the NAMED baseline table (RevTrack Table 1: ours 0.942/0.854 vs RevClassify_DS 0.974/0.953 vs GLASS 0.816/0.705), the discovery recovery result (208 candidates, 5/276=1.8% recall, 121x lift), and a Limitations/Caveats section (different split; F1 at 0.5 threshold; low recall).

tests/test_docs.py: RESULTS.md exists, has the baseline comparison table (mentions RevClassify and GLASS), a caveats/limitations section, and the headline PR-AUC '0.942' string appears in BOTH README and RESULTS (consistency).
bash scripts/verify.sh green
features.notes: ALL numbers come from plans/publishing_facts.md (READ IT FIRST — single source of truth; do not invent metrics). MUST name the source table (RevTrack Table 1, GPU+features) and state the different-split caveat — do not present as identical-split.

features.title: Fix stale docs (data size + superseded banner)
acceptanceCriteria: plan.md and RUNBOOK.md: every wrong '26 GB'/'26GB' data-size mention corrected to '~83 GB extracted / ~24 GB compressed'.
A one-line banner at the top of plan.md noting it is the original design doc, superseded by README.md/RESULTS.md for current results.
tests/test_docs.py: no tracked *.md file contains the substring '26 GB' or '26GB'.
bash scripts/verify.sh green
features.notes: ALL numbers come from plans/publishing_facts.md (READ IT FIRST — single source of truth; do not invent metrics). Only fix the data-size and add the banner; do not rewrite plan.md content.

features.title: pyproject metadata + reproduce commands
acceptanceCriteria: pyproject.toml gains authors, license (MIT), keywords, classifiers, urls; existing deps/config unchanged and still valid.

RUNBOOK.md (or a new REPRODUCE.md) documents the real end-to-end CLI sequence: ingest -> make_split -> build_features -> train_border/score_border -> make_endpoints -> investigate -> train_cluster/score_cluster -> make_typology_signal -> discover_subgraphs -> eval_recovery, plus a short AWS instance/cost note.
tests/test_docs.py or test asserts pyproject parses and has the new [project] keys (authors, keywords, classifiers, urls).
bash scripts/verify.sh green
features.notes: ALL numbers come from plans/publishing_facts.md (READ IT FIRST — single source of truth; do not invent metrics). CLI names are the scripts/*.py basenames. Keep requires-python and dependencies intact.

features.title: CI workflow (verify.sh on push/PR)
acceptanceCriteria: .github/workflows/ci.yml: on push+pull_request, sets up Python 3.11+, creates a venv / installs the package + dev deps, runs `bash scripts/verify.sh`.
tests/test_docs.py: the workflow file exists and is valid YAML (yaml.safe_load succeeds) with a jobs section.
bash scripts/verify.sh green
features.notes: ALL numbers come from plans/publishing_facts.md (READ IT FIRST — single source of truth; do not invent metrics). Mirror the local gate. Use uv if available else pip; keep it minimal.
